query: What is the most recent World Bank / Argus Media monthly price of liquid sulphur Tampa in USD per metric ton for 2026? What were the values month by month January through June 2026?
answer:
## Summary

The question's premise needs correction, which is the headline finding of this research:

**The World Bank Pink Sheet does not publish a sulphur/sulfur price series at all.** I verified this by reading the June 2, 2026 Pink Sheet directly — its fertilizer section contains only DAP, Phosphate rock, Potassium chloride, TSP, and Urea. The actual "Tampa liquid sulphur" benchmark is the **Argus/Green Markets Tampa molten sulphur contract**, which is settled **quarterly** (in $/long tonne CFR), not monthly.

**Month-by-month 2026 (as governed by quarterly contracts):**
- **Jan / Feb / Mar 2026** → Q1 2026 contract = **$495.69/long tonne CFR** (≈ $488/mt), confirmed by Green Markets. Up sharply from $310/lt in Q4 2025. Argus's projected range ($475–520/lt) brackets this settlement.
- **Apr / May / Jun 2026** → Q2 2026 contract. The exact settlement is **behind Argus/Green Markets paywalls and could not be confirmed**, but the trajectory is unambiguously and sharply higher: global benchmarks roughly doubled in H1 2026 (FOB Middle East ~$525→$900/t; CFR China/India to ~$1,050–1,075/t; sulfur CFD hit an all-time high in June 2026).

I did **not** invent a specific Q2/June number since no confirmed public figure exists. Full data points, sources, and access dates have been recorded in the structured output.

key_facts: The World Bank Pink Sheet (CMO) does NOT publish a sulphur/sulfur price series; its fertilizer group is only DAP, Phosphate rock, Potassium chloride, TSP, and Urea (verified in the June 2, 2026 edition).
The relevant benchmark is the Argus/Green Markets 'Tampa molten (liquid) sulphur' contract, settled QUARTERLY in USD per long tonne (lt) CFR/delivered, not a monthly series.
Q1 2026 Tampa molten sulphur contract = $495.69/long tonne CFR (Green Markets), approx $488/mt — this value applies to January, February, and March 2026.
Q4 2025 Tampa contract was $310/lt delivered, so Q1 2026 jumped sharply (+~$185/lt).
Argus projected the Q1 2026 Tampa contract at $475-520/lt delivered; the actual $495.69/lt settlement landed mid-range, cross-validating it.
ChemAnalyst reported North America Gulf Coast sulphur averaged ~$540.00/MT for the quarter ending March 2026, up 25.97% quarter-over-quarter.

Q2 2026 (April-June) Tampa contract settlement is paywalled/unconfirmed publicly, but global sulphur benchmarks rallied sharply: FOB Middle East ~$525/t (Feb) to ~$900/t (June); CFR China ~$546.5 to ~$1,075; CFR India ~$543 to ~$1,050.
World Bank June 2026 commentary noted sulfur prices 'doubled since January' 2026 amid Strait of Hormuz supply disruptions; Trading Economics sulfur CFD hit an all-time high in June 2026.
Unit caution: Tampa is quoted in long tonnes (1 lt ≈ 1.016 mt); the Metaculus question #43612 uses a 250-1500 range implying $/mt or $/lt scale.

data_points.metric: Tampa molten sulphur Q1 2026 contract (covers Jan/Feb/Mar 2026)
